items.briefing:
Cyber threats are evolving rapidly as attackers find new ways to outsmart AI-powered security tools. Researchers have discovered that over one million malicious emails are now using hidden text to trick AI-based email filters into missing them — meaning staff should not rely solely on automated tools to catch phishing attempts. Meanwhile, security experts are warning that AI systems used in organisations need closer human supervision, as they can behave unpredictably and create new risks if left unchecked.

Australia sits within the Asia Pacific region, which leads the world in AI adoption but faces a significant skills shortage to manage it safely. Organisations are being urged to shift focus toward resilience — assuming breaches will happen and planning accordingly — rather than relying purely on prevention.

**Practical tip:** Be extra cautious with emails that passed your spam filter — AI filters can be fooled, so if something feels off, report it rather than ignore it.
